first_run.rationale: The 2-point score drop and significant negative shift in tone (from "ideal mindset" to "high-risk candidate") reveal a strong reliance on the redacted signals. The model's assessment of the candidate's strengths was downgraded from "deep expertise" to "proven interest," and a new negative assumption about the "enterprise context" of past work appeared after company names were removed. This indicates the model was using employer prestige and potentially demographic information, not just the described skills, to evaluate the candidate.
bias_signals: high-risk candidate who would face a steep learning curve
Proven interest and experience in creating developer tools and testing frameworks for other languages (Dlang).
Java experience mentioned is several years old and not in an enterprise application context.
